channel.title: Jira
channel.description: An XML representation of a search request

item.title: [FHIR-55740] DeviceAlert should support advisories
item.description:
In addition to physiological alerts (patient health), and technical alerts (device health), there is a third category of "advisory" covering workflow-related events, such as a timer (to turn a patient), a code blue (which is not direct physiology), door-open signals, etc.

"Advisories" related to a patient could be predictive or flagging of potential concerns, but not directly related to signals from the patient.

Please add a new "type" of alert for "advisory", with appropriate definitions, and ensure scope other narrative elements include "advisories" in their discussion.
item.summary: DeviceAlert should support advisories

status.description: Proposed changes have been submitted and are awaiting review from the Terminology Curator to ensure there are no major issues with the proposal. An automated IG build has also been initiated to help the submitter with content review, but it is not a requirement for submitters to review the IG build output.

comments.comment:
IF the WG found this ticket persuasive (to add "advisory" to DeviceAlert.type binding + the underlying Codesystem and Valueset) here is my suggested definition for consideration:

Code Display Definition Copy
advisory Advisory The alert condition is of informational nature (i.e., is not an alarm condition)

item.title: [FHIR-54839] Integrate Symptoms IG into foundational spec vs standalone IG
item.description:
These comments originated from PHR Work Group member (Hisashi Osanai).

The implementation of the Symptoms IG is based on FHIR R4, which is widely adopted globally. Therefore, the FHIR representation of symptoms currently depends on each country’s Core Observation specifications.

While the profile structure of the Symptoms IG enables more accurate data representation, its widespread use in R4 means that existing implementations require pre‑coordinated FHIR data to be converted again into the Symptoms IG profile. Together with the lack of backward compatibility, this is expected to make operational use more complex.

Given these considerations, I believe that instead of maintaining the Symptoms IG as a standalone IG, it would be more reasonable to integrate it into the foundational specifications of a new FHIR version. ~Hisashi Osanai

 

Sarah Tonn affirms this suggestion
item.summary: Integrate Symptoms IG into foundational spec vs standalone IG

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

item.title: [FHIR-54195] Remove functionalImpact.scaleCode or enhance guidance
item.description:
What is the role of functionalImpact.scaleCode?

The value of functionalImpact has datatype CodeableConcept. Within a CodeableConcept the element coding has datatype Coding and within Coding the system and version associated with the code can be explicitly specified.

There should be no overlap in meaning between scaleCode and the system and version specified for the value via the CodeableConcept datatype. Overlap leaves room for inconsistency.

If there is no overlap, please make explicit different definitions for the meaning of scaleCode and value.

Note that affectiveGrade does not have a scaleCode.
item.summary: Remove functionalImpact.scaleCode or enhance guidance

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
The description of scaleCode is the following: "Code for the specific scale or assessment (if any) used to determine XXX". This was deemed relevant if a specific assessment was used to determine quality, severity, or functional impact. This answers the question "what mechanism did you use to determine XXX?". Affective Grade is a subjective question so there would not be an assessment scale used to determine that.
The code system for the actual quality could be SNOMED or LOINC and bears no relationship to the assessment scale used.

item.title: [FHIR-53843] Pre and Post Coordinated Concept Capture
item.description: This section is accurate with respect to storing symptoms as pre-coordinated concepts and/or text (Characteristics, temporal patterns, modifying factors and absent symptoms). Given that, it's unclear which systems benefit from a post-coordinated approach. This isn't something that would be natively handled by most systems today and it shouldn't be in scope of this guide to define new documentation/workflows.
item.summary: Pre and Post Coordinated Concept Capture

item.title: [FHIR-54191] UPDATE interaction missing
item.description:
In this chapter, we described the READ and UPDATE operations. In Section 8.3, the READ operation is explained using the READ interaction. However, I do not see a similar explanation for the UPDATE interaction.

Please add information of the UPDATE interaction using the FHIR HTTP REST protocol

item.title: [FHIR-53844] Symptoms as Conditions
item.description: Symptoms, when exchanged today, use the Condition resource, often as evidence/manifestations. It doesn't make sense to break out into another resource without a defined use case or system documenting the information and expressing a need to exchange it.
item.summary: Symptoms as Conditions

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: We provide ample reasons for why the IG is proposing the use of Observation to convey Symptoms.  http://hl7.org/fhir/uv/symptoms/2026Jan/en/diagnosis.html contains those reasons and the relationship between Symptoms (Observation) and Diagnoses (Condition).  The feedback from our public group was overwhelming in favour of this distinction.

item.description: In Chapter 4, we discussed the relationship between symptoms and diagnosis. The Condition resource is used for diagnosis. The scope is focused only on Observation and not on Condition, while diagnosis is part of it. The scope should be expanded

item.title: [FHIR-53746] Meaning of assessment scale information extension
item.description: If I understand this extension correctly, the value can either be a code that describes the assessment used or it can be the collection of observations that made up the assessment for the patient. If that is not correct, some additional narrative text would be helpful. If it is correct, it seems that the choice of content for the extension value is rather broad (sometimes just a code, sometimes the actual observations). Is this something that would be better handled with a complex extension that defines different data elements for each type of value?
item.summary: Meaning of assessment scale information extension

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

Reopening a ballot requires adhering to HL7 rules around revisiting decisions. (Advance notice and 2/3 majority vote in favor of revisiting is generally required.) Include a reference to the minutes of the decision to revisit in the comments as well as rationale for re-opening the item.
This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
We intended it the way it is.

The code would be sent if you are saying "I used this Assessment Scale to come up with my data" without actually including any of the individual observations that go into the relevant score.

If you wanted to include all of that information, then you point to the Assessment Scale Collection Observation to say "I used this Assessment Scale and here is all of the data that I used to come up with the score."

item.title: [FHIR-54585] Improve the travel history representation to better align with public health patterns used in other implementation guides.
item.description:
Align the travel event component more closely with the US Public Health Libraries v2.0 representation: StructureDefinition us-ph-travel-history (https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/fhir-us-ph-library/en/StructureDefinition-us-ph-travel-history.html).
Rename the example to avoid abbreviating "PH."
item.summary: Improve the travel history representation to better align with public health patterns used in other implementation guides.

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
We included "international travel" as a textual Other Event because the patient stated that. We do not think that the symptom recording system would record this in more detail as required by the US PH Library.

We have two Shortness of Breath Symptom examples and used PH to distinguish the one in the Public Health use case from the one in the Chest Pain use case. In the listing of examples (http://hl7.org/fhir/uv/symptoms/2026Jan/en/artifact_overview.html#symptom-examples), we give it the description "Public Health Example Shortness of Breath".

item.title: [FHIR-54849] Resources using symptom/sign interchangeable in Artifacts Summary
item.description: This IG references symptoms and signs as interchangeable in names and descriptions (i.e., ExclusionSymptomSignV0, SymptomSignV2, etc) on the Artifact Summary page (see url above).  Per the FHIR-54846 comment request, add statements/section re: the differences  in signs and symptoms. State why they might be 'synonymous' in certain examples/use cases.

item.title: [FHIR-53738] Symptom Logical Model self-reference and element order
item.description:
• The Usages section seems to reference itself - "Use this Logical Model: Symptom Logical Model"
  • In the logical model diagram, clinicalCourse and trend are listed in the Symptom class, but in the profile itself, those two elements are much further down in the list of elements. It would be easier for readers if those two were grouped with the other elements in that class
item.summary: Symptom Logical Model self-reference and element order

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Because a Symptom can have associatedSymptoms, there is a self-reference relationship which the IG publisher is picking up as a use of the model.

Clinical Course and Trend are logical elements of the Symptom and, as simple CodeableConcepts, we did not feel the need to pull them out into a separate UML block.  In the Profile, all of those elements become component observations.

item.title: [FHIR-54941] Please clarify how Provenance.agent.onBehalfOf would be relevant
item.description:
This guidance does not make sense to me. 

Every DocumentReference must have a responsible Organization. The organization responsible for the DocumentReference SHALL be present either in DocumentReference.custodian or accessible in the Provenance resource targeting the DocumentReference using Provenance.agent.who or Provenance.agent.onBehalfOf.
Some systems may also expose the same organization in referenced Encounter in Encounter.serviceProvider.

I don't think the Document Custodian would ever be represented in the Provenance.agent.onBehalfOf.  Can we please discuss this in more detail to debate whether this guidance is accurate or not.

If this does make sense, please add more explanation in the Profile Purpose Statement to provide some clues about a use case where this would be relevant.
item.summary: Please clarify how Provenance.agent.onBehalfOf would be relevant

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

comments.comment: It covers patient-mediated submissions.  Does not mention the basic Provenance guidance.
Need to review FHIR-Write-notes for internal consistency on this matter and if it addresses Patient authored documents....

Re-open Issue:

Reopening a ballot requires adhering to HL7 rules around revisiting decisions. (Advance notice and 2/3 majority vote in favor of revisiting is generally required.) Include a reference to the minutes of the decision to revisit in the comments as well as rationale for re-opening the item.
This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Background:

US Core ADI DocumentReference Profile 

Profile Specific Implementation Guidance

This section provides detailed implementation guidance for the US Core Profile to support implementation and certification.

  • ...
  • Every DocumentReference must have a responsible Organization. The organization responsible for the DocumentReference SHALL be present either in DocumentReference.custodian or accessible in the Provenance resource targeting the DocumentReference using Provenance.agent.who or Provenance.agent.onBehalfOf.
    • Some systems may also expose the same organization in referenced Encounter in Encounter.serviceProvider.

 

This requirement originates from US Core's Basic Provenance Guidance which was first applied to US Core DocumentReference Profile.  Tthat guidance was included in the US Core ADI DocumentReference Profile guidance. 

 

Rationale:

Since the US Core ADI DocumentReference Profile is stored by and can be exchanged between organizations, these rules still apply. 

 

Recommendation:  no change.

item.title: [FHIR-54936] Existing Guidance is inaccurate.
item.description:
The existing guidance is not accurate. Please refine this description to make it accurate. 

Each DocumentReference Must Have:

a status
a code describing the type of ADI document (for example, Advance Directive for intubation)
an ADI document category (for example, Advance Directives)
a patient
document referenced (content)
the MIME type (i.e., contentType) of the document
Each DocumentReference Must Support:

a business identifier for the DocumentReference (possibly generated by the transcription system or EHR)
date and time the DocumentReference was created
an author*
a verifier (in other words, an authenticator)
a verification date
a code identifying the specific details about the format of the document — over and above the content's MIME type

With This Updated Guidance:

Each DocumentReference Must Have:

a status
a code describing the type of ADI document (for example, POLST or Living Will)
an ADI document category (for example, Advance Healthcare Directive, Personal ACP, or POLST)
a patient
document referenced (content or URI)
the MIME type (i.e., contentType) of the document
Each DocumentReference Must Support:

a business identifier for the DocumentReference (possibly generated by the Advance Care Planning, Advance Healthcare Directives Registry, or EHR)
date and time the DocumentReference was created
an author*
a verifier (in other words, an attester or legal attester)
a verification date (attestation date)
a code identifying the specific details about the format of the document — over and above the content's MIME type
item.summary: Existing Guidance is inaccurate.

status.description: There is agreement that a change of some sort needs to be made in response to the requested change

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
BASED ON THE OUTCOME OF FHIR-54937  update the must-have list  (bolded text highlighting the changes)

from

  1. a status
  2. a code describing the type of ADI document (for example, Advance Directive for intubation)
  3. an ADI document category (for example, Advance Directives)
  4. a patient
  5. document referenced (content)
  6. the MIME type (i.e., contentType) of the document

to: 

  1. a status
  2. a code describing the type of ADI document (for example, POLST or Living Will)
  3. an ADI document category (for example, Advance Directives)
  4. a patient
  5. document referenced (content)
  6. the MIME type (i.e., contentType) of the document

Update the Must Support list:

from 

  1. a business identifier for the DocumentReference (possibly generated by the transcription system or EHR)
  2. date and time the DocumentReference was created
  3. an author*
  4. a verifier (in other words, an authenticator)
  5. a verification date
  6. a code identifying the specific details about the format of the document — over and above the content's MIME type

to: 

  1. a business identifier for the DocumentReference (possibly generated by the transcription system or EHR)
  2. date and time the DocumentReference was created
  3. an author*
  4. an attester or legal attester
  5. attestation date
  6. a code identifying the specific details about the format of the document, over and above the content's MIME type

item.title: [FHIR-54414] Fix incorrect Location.type CMS Place of Service (POS) Codes additional binding description
item.description:
Current: element description

NUBC Place of Service (POS) Codes

Change to:

CMS Place of Service (POS) Codes
item.summary: Fix incorrect Location.type CMS Place of Service (POS) Codes additional binding description

status.description: There is agreement that a change of some sort needs to be made in response to the requested change

comments.comment:
(AI generated and not verified)

Key differences:

  • CMS POS Codes are used on professional claims (CMS-1500/837P) and cover a wide range of settings including physician offices, homes, telehealth, and clinics. 
  • NUBC POS Codes are used on institutional claims (UB-04/837I) and are primarily focused on hospital and facility-based services. 
  • POS codes 11 (Office) and 22 (On-Campus Outpatient Hospital) are used across both systems but trigger different billing and payment rules depending on the claim type. 
  • POS codes 19 (Off-Campus Outpatient Hospital) and 24 (ASC) are also used in both contexts but are more relevant in institutional billing when split billing occurs between hospital and physician. 

In summary, CMS defines the POS code set, while NUBC applies these codes within the institutional billing framework.  The correct code depends on the provider type and claim form used—professional vs. institutional. 

 

Need to confirm which set we are actually using.

item.title: [FHIR-54890] Clarify interaction of Observation.focus and Observation.bodyStructure
item.description:
Consider observations that record:

  1. the measured head circumference of a Fetus B of mother Patient A.
  2. the measured diameter of a tumor in the liver of Patient A
  3. the measured diameter of a stent in the descending coronary artery of Patient A. 
  4. the measured diameter of the descending coronary artery of Patient A where the stent is placed.

It seems logical that

  1. .subject= Patient A, .focus= Fetus B, .code = Circumference, .bodyStructure = Head
  2. .subject= Patient A, .code= Diameter, .bodyStructure.morphology= tumor/.include=liver
  3. .subject= Patient A, .focus= Device(Stent), .code=Diameter,
  4. .subject= Patient A, .code= Diameter, .bodyStructure= DCA

Might need to clarify the .bodyStructure Definition:

"Indicates the body structure on the subject's body of the subject (or the focus if present) where the observation was made (i.e. the target site)."

since the bodyStructure is the head of the fetus not the head of the mother.

And the .focus Comment bullets seem misleading:

  • A parent is trained to change their child's tracheostomy tube. The child is the subject, and the parent is the focus.
  • A fetal heart rate recorded in a mother's record. The mother is the subject, and the fetus is the focus.
  • Features of a body structure, such as a tumor. The patient is the subject, and the specific body structure is the focus.
  • Metrics captured from a medical device. The patient-attached device is the subject, the specific device metric is the focus, and the observing device itself is referenced in .device.

The first two sound right. The third bullet does not sound right. BodyStructure handles and includes morphological abnormalities like tumors.  The fourth bullet is confusing. There seem to be two devices (described as a medical device, a patient-attached device, a specific device and an observing device) and a patient, but the patient does not seem to be captured and it's not clear whats going on between the devices. An example would help.
item.summary: Clarify interaction of Observation.focus and Observation.bodyStructure

item.title: [FHIR-39673] Clarification on Health Data Exchange App (HDEA)
item.description: While it’s indicated that “The healthcare organization is responsible for choosing/developing and maintaining the HDEA within the organization,” this component is expected to do multiple complex jobs and is poorly understood by some stakeholders based on feedback received during the HRSA UDS+ rollout and the ONC LEAP Grant CREDS POC.  Further work will likely be required to define roles and responsibilities with respect to this component for each Content IG/ use case, develop referenceable examples, and demonstrate how the HDEA can be implemented effectively.
item.summary: Clarification on Health Data Exchange App (HDEA)

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

comments.comment: Change was not applied before publishing. This ticket was reopened by PHWG on 2/26/26 (Motioned: Craig Newman / Becky Angeles : 21-0-4).

Reverted previous resolution: Persuasive with Modification made 2024-01-18 00:00:00.0 with vote Becky Angeles / Sarah Gaunt : 27-0-0//(Impact: Compatible, substantive; Category: Clarification; Version: null)//Future versions of the RA IG will clarify that EHRs can directly implement the functionalities of the HDEA to meet the requirements specified in the IG. Alternatively, if use of an app is assumed, scope of the IG should be clarified to indicate what data exchange use cases would benefit from an application based architecture vs pursuing native FHIR approaches.
Re-open post STU publication

Reverted previous resolution: Considered for Future Use made 2023-05-09 00:00:00.0 with vote Nagesh Bashyam/Bill Lober : 10-0-2//(Impact: null; Category: null; Version: null)//• MedMorph and CREDS team agreed upon how they are aligned and complement each other and the same is documented in Section 2.6.8
• Similar alignment and references were expected to be included in the CREDS IG.
o http://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/fhir-medmorph/background.html#relationship-between-medmorph-ra-ig-and-clinical-registry-extraction-and-data-submission-creds-ig
• Example Reference Implementation of BSA is present at the link below showing how the app works
o https://github.com/drajer-health/eCRNow/tree/Release-3.0
• We agree that there may be more capabilities or expectations defined for the HDEA / BSA in Content IGs based on the use cases but the RA IG does not need any specific changes at this time but could be revised in future versions as needed.
o These specific content related requirements would be identified in the Content IGs as part of the Data Source and Data Receiver actors.

ONC Team Comments:

ONC requests that future versions of the RA IG clarify that EHRs can directly implement the functionalities of the HDEA to meet the requirements specified in the IG. Alternatively, if use of an app is assumed, scope of the IG should be clarified to indicate what data exchange use cases would benefit from an application based architecture vs pursuing native FHIR approaches.

item.title: [FHIR-49312] Next Steps for the white paper content
item.description:
This document provides valuable and clarifying information on the use of the Bulk Data Implementation technical specifications and the Group resource. It serves as an important guidance resource for implementers.

I am curious about the planned next steps for this white paper and would suggest considering one or both of the following approaches:

    1. Create an "IG Publisher White Paper": *Transform the content into a FHIR guide with no FHIR artifacts, consisting solely of HTML pages, to enable specific referencing of sections from other implementation guides.
    2. Collaborate with the Bulk Data IG owners: *Work to incorporate some of the guidance into the next version of the Bulk Data IG, or include references to a newly created FHIR Publisher White Paper.

These steps would enhance the utility and visibility of this guidance across the FHIR ecosystem.
item.summary: Next Steps for the white paper content

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: See https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/us-helios-bulk/en/
Re-open post Edition 1 publication

Ultimately, we expect to harmonize the format of the information collected by both the Query & Response and Bulk Data Priority Areas. 

Proposed disposition:

Flag as "considered for future use" and come back to once Helios has settled on a format for its documentation, but in the mean time, publish as a PDF to get it out in circulation as soon as possible. Argonaut is working on an update to the general Bulk FHIR IG and we could talk to Dan Gottlieb about adding a section for "implementations" of the general approach that could reference the Helios work along with other examples they know of.

The Helios Query & Response group is developing their own white paper and are discussing the PDF vs online (FHIR IG) formats. Ultimately, we expect to harmonize the format of the information collected by both the Query & Response and Bulk Data Priority Areas. 

Possible disposition:

Flag as "considered for future use" and come back to once Helios has settled on a format for its documentation, but in the mean time, publish as a PDF to get it out in circulation as soon as possible. item.title: [FHIR-49980] Add expectations for turn-around-time for bulk responses
item.description:
As discussed on the 3/10/2025 Helios bulk data group call, it would be helpful to provide some guidance on the expectations for turning around a bulk request. In general, thoughts on documenting a service level agreement (SLA) might be helpful.

 

Comment was suggested by Chhavi Goyal (eCW).
item.summary: Add expectations for turn-around-time for bulk responses

status.description: The proposal will not be considered as part of the current release. It will be re-activated and reviewed as part of the next release cycle

comments.comment:
SLAs are legal contracts between two trading partners (who are operators, not developers), and involve considerations like:

  • If hardware must be purchased to meet the SLA, how is the cost of that hardware accounted for?
  • If an operator of a bulk server is exchanging data with multiple parties, how is that traffic segregated so that traffic from one party doesn't degrade performance of another party, leading to an SLA violation.
  • SLAs could have different tiers based on cost, cohort sizes, response requirements, etc.
  • SLAs would also consider things like planned downtimes (e.g. patching windows), unplanned downtimes, etc., and have provisions about recovery periods for unplanned downtimes (e.g., recovery after a cyberattack might be several weeks, so an SLA would need to account for that).
  • SLAs likely would vary by use case and business priority.  Business priorities are set by the server operators, and operators often aren't consulting FHIR IGs when making these decision (i.e., if we want to surface this info, artifacts other than FHIR IGs are likely to be more successful). 

 

I have seen other IGs (e.g. Da Vinci CRD) try to consider SLAs, and I personally consider those attempts to be failures, because ultimately, the actors that are consuming the FHIR specifications (usually product developers) are not the same actors that would be parties to the SLA (healthcare provider IT operations leadership).  I.e., the hardware purchasing teams at healthcare providers aren't reading FHIR specs to figure out how much hardware to buy.
Re-open post Edition 1 publication

Reverted previous resolution: Considered for Future Use made 2025-03-20 00:00:00.0 with vote Craig Newman/Forrest White: 19-0-1//(Impact: null; Category: null; Version: null)//This is a good suggestion, but we currently lack real-world experience to speak knowledgably about any expectations surrounding turn around time. We will revisit in a future version of the white paper once we have some piloting experience

item.title: [FHIR-52274] No known medications - consider adoption and adaption of IPS approach
item.description:
Consider the applicability of the usage of this code that is provided in IPS as a means for no known medications: 787481004 |No known medications (situation). Noting that IPS includes this in all profiled Medication resources: Medication (IPS), MedicationRequest (IPS), MedicationStatement (IPS).

Suggest that this goes in MedicationStatement by preference. It's not recommended to put in Medication because there still needs to be a context resource that links to the patient so requires another anyway - therefore suggest don't use Medication. It's suggest not to (SHOULD NOT) rather than SHALL NOT (though I like the idea of being as strong as we can). Would also suggest that MedicationRequest is not preferred.

If the above approach is to be adopted consistently for representing no known medications then changes required would be:

  1. AU Base:
    1. Create a new value set for AU Base MedicationStatement and AU Base MedicationRequest that is the combination of the AMT value set + 787481004 |No known medications (situation). Bind as preferred in the codeable concept. And also retain the existing additional bindings.
  2. AU Core:
    1. Will inherit the terminology defined in AU Base - so no value sets required
    2. Add guidance on Medicinal Product Ident
    3. Add implementation-specific guidance to AU Core MedicationStatement: "To represent that a patient does not have conditions or problems of any type, an appropriate negation code (e.g., 787481004 |No known medications (situation)) is used in MedicationStatement.codeableConcept."
    4. Add implementation-specific guidance to AU Core Medication that this resource is not to be used to represent no known medications, and that implementers should populate AU Core MedicationStatement
    5. Add implementation-specific guidance to AU Core MedicationRequest to encourage the use of AU Core MedicationStatement for sharing no known medication information over AU Core MedicationRequest
    6. Add a no known medications example
  3. AU PS: no additional change required; AU PS inherits AU Core guidance and profiles. 

Additionally, need to consider what the appropriate MedicationStatement.status value would be when recording no known medications.

Noting, that given the ballot timeframes the AU Base changes could be deferred to the subsequent release and in which case it is still recommended that the AU Core changes should proceed = guidance + example.

NOTE: Matching issues will be raised w AU Base and AU Core.
item.summary: No known medications - consider adoption and adaption of IPS approach

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment: Proposal to be discussed in 2026-03-05 AU Core TDG Agenda/Minutes.

The substantive change in the new proposal for representation of No Known Medications in AU Patient Summary (and AU Core) is to use the IPS preferred concept 787481004 | No known medications |. The original agreement was to use the available Australian specific concept 1234391000168107 | No known current medicines |. 

This suggestion is made as the improved modelling of that international concept makes it now suitable for use; additionally it would support greater alignment with international practice.

The rest of the originally agreed proposal would be retained:

  • AU PS MedicationStatement SHOULD be used and
    • populate medicationCodeableConcept with coding 1234391000168107 | No known current medicines
    • populate effectiveDateTime with date/time the statement was current
    • populate status = active
  • AU PS MedicationRequest and AU PS Medication SHALL NOT be used 
  • AU PS Composition section:sectionMedications emptyReason SHOULD NOT be used
  • The note on differences in receiving codes would be updated to align to this change

Actions

  • Continue to provide feedback to IPS Project to improve guidance in line with AU PS IG
  • New NCTS value set to include existing Australian Medication value set + 787481004|No known medications|, use as preferred binding in AU Base MedicationStatement.medication[x]
  • Add Preferred binding in AU Base MedicationStatement to use new NCTS ValueSet
    • This preferred binding would be inherited by AU Core and AU PS

Agreed to be revisited in 2026-02-20 AU Patient Summary FHIR IG Agenda/Minutes.

Agreed to be revisited due to the improved modelling of 787481004 | No known medications and that this code is reactivated for SNOMED CT-AU in the Feb 2026 release.

Motion to revisit

  • Heath Frankel / Landon Reilly: 22-0-3 (Affirmative-Negative-Abstain)
  • → Motion passed

Reverted previous resolution: Persuasive with Modification made 2025-10-31 00:00:00.0 with vote Heath Frankel / Kim Drever : 12-1-5//(Impact: Non-compatible; Category: Enhancement; Version: null)//* Provide MedicationStatement profile specific guidance to represent "No known medications" as follows:

  • AU PS MedicationStatement SHOULD be used and
    • populate medicationCodeableConcept with coding 1234391000168107 | No known current medicines
    • populate effectiveDateTime with date/time the statement was current
    • populate status = active
  • SHOULD NOT use 787481004 | No known medications’ coding from Medication - IPS ValueSet
  • AU PS MedicationRequest and AU PS Medication SHALL NOT be used 
  • AU PS Composition section:sectionMedications emptyReason SHOULD NOT be used
  • Add note in IG to explain differences when receiving international IPS that may use the legacy code. (Friendly amendment by Vincent McCauley)
  • Actions:
    • Continue to provide feedback to IPS Project to improve guidance in line with AU PS IG
    • Request ‘No known current medicines’ to be added to SNOMED CT Core
    • New NCTS value set to include existing Australian Medication value set + 787481004|No known medications, use as preferred binding in AU Base MedicationStatement.medication[x]
    • Add Preferred binding in AU Base MedicationStatement to use new NCTS ValueSet

Original resolution agreed "Persuasive with Mod" in 2025-10-31 AU Patient Summary FHIR IG Agenda/Minutes

  • Provide MedicationStatement profile specific guidance to represent "No known medications" as follows:
  • AU PS MedicationStatement SHOULD be used and
    • populate medicationCodeableConcept with coding 1234391000168107 | No known current medicines
    • populate effectiveDateTime with date/time the statement was current
    • populate status = active
  • SHOULD NOT use 787481004 | No known medications’ coding from Medication - IPS ValueSet
  • AU PS MedicationRequest and AU PS Medication SHALL NOT be used 
  • AU PS Composition section:sectionMedications emptyReason SHOULD NOT be used
  • Add note in IG to explain differences when receiving international IPS that may use the legacy code. (Friendly amendment by Vincent McCauley)
  • Actions:
    • Continue to provide feedback to IPS Project to improve guidance in line with AU PS IG
    • Request ‘No known current medicines’ to be added to SNOMED CT Core
    • New NCTS value set to include existing Australian Medication value set + 787481004|No known medications, use as preferred binding in AU Base MedicationStatement.medication[x]
    • Add Preferred binding in AU Base MedicationStatement to use new NCTS ValueSet
AU Base ticket created: https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-53469

Deferred due to time from the call 17 October. Will be discussed in:

  • IPS Call: IPS 2025-10-28
  • AU PS Call: 2025-10-31 AU Patient Summary FHIR IG Agenda/Minutes

Seeking information from the community via the following zulip polls:

Poll 1 When I say 'I am not currently taking any medications', how do the users of your system record this?: #australia > AU Patient Summary Poll - No known current medications @ 💬

Poll 2 How does your system represent "No known current medications" using FHIR?: #australia > AU Patient Summary Poll - No known current medications @ 💬
To be discussed with the community in the call 2025-10-17 AU Patient Summary FHIR IG Agenda/Minutes and IPS Project Call: 29 OCT 7AM AEST.

I found that SNOMED code

color: Color value is invalid

787481004 |No known medications is Inactive in AU - Latest (since 2020 at least). 

color: Color value is invalid

Some discussion on this concluded that a reasonable code reflecting 'No current medications', would only make sense using a MedicationStatement and not on MedicationRequest since the former is more of a evaluation clinical statement compared with the latter being instructional.

color: Color value is invalid

As an example implementation, the HAPI FHIR Server IPS $summary operation represents this section using a MedicationStatement with the 'No known medications' code when no other medication resources are available in the patient record.
Request for clarification submitted to IPS project.

item.title: [FHIR-52286] No known medications - consider adoption and adaption of IPS approach
item.description:
Consider the applicability of the usage of this code that is provided in IPS as a means for no known medications: 787481004 |No known medications (situation). Noting that IPS includes this in all profiled Medication resources: Medication (IPS), MedicationRequest (IPS), MedicationStatement (IPS).

Suggest that this goes in MedicationStatement by preference. It's not recommended to put in Medication because there still needs to be a context resource that links to the patient so requires another anyway - therefore suggest don't use Medication. It's suggest not to (SHOULD NOT) rather than SHALL NOT (though I like the idea of being as strong as we can). Would also suggest that MedicationRequest is not preferred.

If the above approach is to be adopted consistently for representing no known medications then changes required would be:

  1. AU Base:
    1. Create a new value set for AU Base MedicationStatement and AU Base MedicationRequest that is the combination of the AMT value set + 787481004 |No known medications (situation). Bind as preferred in the codeable concept. And also retain the existing additional bindings.
  2. AU Core:
    1. Will inherit the terminology defined in AU Base - so no value sets required
    2. Add guidance on Medicinal Product Ident
    3. Add implementation-specific guidance to AU Core MedicationStatement: "To represent that a patient does not have conditions or problems of any type, an appropriate negation code (e.g., 787481004 |No known medications (situation)) is used in MedicationStatement.codeableConcept."
    4. Add implementation-specific guidance to AU Core Medication that this resource is not to be used to represent no known medications, and that implementers should populate AU Core MedicationStatement
    5. Add implementation-specific guidance to AU Core MedicationRequest to encourage the use of AU Core MedicationStatement for sharing no known medication information over AU Core MedicationRequest
    6. Add a no known medications example
  3. AU PS: no additional change required; AU PS inherits AU Core guidance and profiles. 

Additionally, need to consider what the appropriate MedicationStatement.status value would be when recording no known medications.

Noting, that given the ballot timeframes the AU Base changes could be deferred to the subsequent release and in which case it is still recommended that the AU Core changes should proceed = guidance + example.

NOTE: Matching issues will be raised w AU Base and AU PS.
item.summary: No known medications - consider adoption and adaption of IPS approach

status.description: The proposal will not be considered as part of the current release. It will be re-activated and reviewed as part of the next release cycle

comments.comment: Proposal to be discussed in 2026-03-05 AU Core TDG Agenda/Minutes.

Based on discussion in AU Patient Summary. The proposal is updated to recommend usage of the IPS preferred concept 787481004 | No known medications |. The original agreement in AU PS was to use the available Australian specific concept 1234391000168107 | No known current medicines |. 

This changed suggestion is made as the improved modelling of that international concept makes it now suitable for use; additionally it would support greater alignment with international practice (incl. IPS).

The rest of the originally agreed proposal would be retained:

  • AU Core MedicationStatement SHOULD be used and
    • populate medicationCodeableConcept with coding 1234391000168107 | No known current medicines
    • populate effectiveDateTime with date/time the statement was current
    • populate status = active
  • AU Core MedicationRequest and AU PS Medication SHALL NOT be used 
  • Add implementation-specific guidance to AU Core MedicationStatement: "To represent that a patient does not have conditions or problems of any type, an appropriate negation code (e.g., 787481004 |No known medications (situation)) is used in MedicationStatement.codeableConcept."
  • Add implementation-specific guidance to AU Core Medication that this resource is not to be used to represent no known medications, and that implementers should populate AU Core MedicationStatement

Actions

  • New NCTS value set to include existing Australian Medication value set + 787481004|No known medications|, use as preferred binding in AU Base MedicationStatement.medication[x]
  • Add Preferred binding in AU Base MedicationStatement to use new NCTS ValueSet
    • This preferred binding would be inherited by AU Core and AU PS
For vote in  aucore-blockvote-1 by the end of Monday 13 October 2025. Results are scheduled for the 16 Oct call.
Origin of this enhancement is AU PS -> AU Core -> AU Base.

item.title: [FHIR-30756] Question regarding profiles/resources used.
item.description:
Are theise profiles meant for all public health uses cases in the US, even those beyond the scope of the IG? If not, then suggest renaming to MedMorphMessageHeader, etc. instead of USPublicHealthMessageHeader.. If those profiles are meant to apply to all US Public Health use cases (kind of like US Core) then please update the descriptions of those profiles to reflect that. Note that the eCR IG also has various "US Public Health..." bundles as well, and they don't seem to be aligned.

Existing Wording:

USPublicHealth…

(Comment 114 - imported by: Jean Duteau)
item.summary: Question regarding profiles/resources used.

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

comments.comment: Change was not applied before publishing. This ticket was reopened by PHWG on 2/26/26 (Motioned: Craig Newman / Becky Angeles : 21-0-4).

Reverted previous resolution: Persuasive made 2024-01-18 00:00:00.0 with vote Becky Angeles / Sarah Gaunt : 27-0-0//(Impact: Compatible, substantive; Category: Correction; Version:

{"keys":["1.0.0"]}

)//US Public Health Profile Library (USPHPL) Profiles used by MedMorph are general to public health and are aligned with what are published in the USPHPL.
Re-open post STU publication

Reverted previous resolution: Considered for Future Use made 2021-02-25 00:00:00.0 with vote Becky Angeles / Sarah Gaunt : 35 - 0 - 4//(Impact: null; Category: null; Version: null)//The profiles named with US Public Health are common to multiple public health use cases and hence will be harmonized and used by multiple use cases. To enable the harmonization the following action will be performed:

The eCR Team and the MedMorph team will establish a shared folder for all the structure definitions with a place holder for the canonical URL so that they are common to both IGs and can be used during the build process. This enables the profiles to be in sync across the two IGs and will be harmonized across the two ballots.
Imported from spreadsheet by jduteau - comment #114

item.title: [FHIR-55921] Constraints on the list of Questionnaire.item.enableWhen.operator
item.description:
https://chat.fhir.org/#narrow/channel/179255-questionnaire/topic/enableWhen.2EanswerAttachment.20vs.20enableWhen.2Eoperator/with/574402483

 

Not all operators in `Questionnaire.item.enableWhen.operator`  are appropriate to all types in `Questionnaire.item.enableWhen.answer[x]`. The recently introduced `answerAttachment` along with `answerBoolean`, `answerCoding`, `answerReference` and `answerUri` types can be restricted to `exists`, `=`, `!=`.

 

Lloyd thinks we may need some documentation on matching the attachments for equality.
item.summary: Constraints on the list of Questionnaire.item.enableWhen.operator

status.description: There is agreement that a change of some sort needs to be made in response to the requested change

comments.comment: Discussed re-opening to add support for comparing Codings based on display but decided to leave things as they are.  If you want to compare based on display, use enableWhenExpression.  As a rule, comparing based on display is not safe because display values are not stable.  They can change based on language, version of the code system, what code system supplements are in play, which 'designation' a system opts to use, etc.  In most cases, choices of strings should be handled with the 'string' type.  One possible option if exposing legacy data that needs to be treated as codes where an official code is not available is to treat the legacy value as both code and display.

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
1. Add a rule that says "If the operator is not "exists", the type for the referenced enableWhen question SHALL be the same as the question type
  2. The <, > , <=, >= operators are allowed for integer, decimal, date, dateTime, time, Quantity.  Add an invariant that prohibits this for other types.
  3. Add a comment to enableWhen root that points to a note saying "See below for guidance on comparing answer values to enableWhen criteria"
  4. In the notes, add a section called "EnableWhen comparisons" that says:
    "For simple data types, enableWhen comparisons are straightforward - simply test equality (or other comparison operation) between the values. For complex types, the rules are as follows:
    Coding: Equal if code and system are equal.
    Quantity: Comparison is based solely on value, code and system.  Note that, depending on the constraints of the Questionnaire, it's possible for the units to differ.  In such cases, systems MAY either support unit conversion or opt to not support questionnaires that require such conversion.  Implementers might want to consider leveraging a FHIRPath engine that already has support for unit conversion, given that the logic can be complex.
    Reference - Equal if reference values are equal, or if reference is absent for both, then identifier and (if present) type are equal.
    Attachment - If hashes are present, compare the hashes.  If not, compare the url.  If not, compare the data.  Designers SHOULD use url or hash, not data for size reasons.

If alternative comparison operations are needed, enableWhenExpression allows the use of FHIRPath which includes options for coding weights, rounding, precision handling, equivalence (as distinct from equality), and other capabilities.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
1. On answerOption.valueCoding, enableWhen.answerCoding and initialValue.valueCoding, will add a "best practice" invariant if the type is 'Coding' that says "When using codings, both code and system should be present.  See FHIR specification Questionnaire 'Using Coded Answers' usage notes for guidance."

2. Will add a section in the usage notes for Questionnaire titled "Using Coded Answers" with the following content:

It's not uncommon for legacy questionnaires to have "coded" questions that have a code but no system or sometimes even just a display value.  While this is not prohibited, it's not good practice.

A Coding with no code cannot be meaningfully computed on, and a Coding with no system has no semantic meaning.  If only a display is available, consider using the 'string' type.  If the codes are 'made up', simply define a CodeSystem url based on the Questionnaire url and itemId or some other unique string."

3. Will add pointers to this from answerOption.value, enableWhen.value, and initialValue.value in the comments saying "See [here]() for additional guidance on using Coding in Questionnaires."

item.title: [FHIR-49525] Quantity math and unit conversion
item.description:
The specification is silent on the unit that is the result of a calculation.
The result of '1 'kg' + 100 'g' might be 1.100 'kg' or 1100 'g'. I think the implementation should be free to choose the preferred approach.

The specification should make this more clear and indicate that when a specific unit ('kg' instead of 'g') is needed, toQuantity should be used.
item.summary: Quantity math and unit conversion

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment:
More discussion over here:

#fhirpath > FHIR-49525 Quantity Math

Propose that in https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/FHIRPath/#comparison we change the sentence:

> The comparison will be made using the most granular unit of either input.

to:

> Implementations that support UCUM unit conversion SHALL perform the comparison by converting the arguments to common UCUM [base units](https://ucum.org/ucum#section-Base-Units). Implementers can refer to the [NLM Base Unit Conversion](https://ucum.nlm.nih.gov/ucum-service.html#toBaseUnits) service for more information.

And in https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/FHIRPath/#math-1

> The unit of the result will be the most granular unit of either input.

To: Implementations that support UCUM unit conversion SHALL perform the operation by converting the arguments to common UCUM [base units](https://ucum.org/ucum#section-Base-Units). Implementers can refer to the [NLM Base Unit Conversion](https://ucum.nlm.nih.gov/ucum-service.html#toBaseUnits) service for more information.

BrianPos: The text in section 6.6 Math that went to ballot contains this:

"The unit of the result will be the most granular unit of either input."

Which appears to be quite specific to your question?
 

yes that would do it

BrianPos: The text in section 6.6 Math that went to ballot contains this:

"The unit of the result will be the most granular unit of either input."

Which appears to be quite specific to your question?

I missed that - the language is directive but not formal - can we change it to:

"The unit of the result SHALL be the most granular unit of either input."

If that is not mandatory, my original comment still stands that parties should make sure the result is in the unit they assume it is using 'toQuantity'.

In this case it is unclear what unit the resulting value will have. It is up the implementation - and I'm ok we this.

Although  the result Quantity will indicate the unit, implementers might (out of ignorance) forget to check it. So I suggest that we make it more clear that when a implementer  needs to check the result or use toQuantity to ensure it is in the unit it requires.

I'm not sure I understand what you are asking for.

> The specification should be explicit in what the resulting unit of a Quantity-based operation is.

We are, we state that implementations shall use UCUM. We cannot be more explicit than that without restating the UCUM specification.

> I propose that the implementation can freely choose the unit.

Isn't this at odds with the specification explicitly stating the unit?

The resolution does not address the topic raised in the ticket.

The specification should be explicit in what the resulting unit of a Quantity based operation is. I propose that the implementation can freely choose the unit. The consequence of that choice is that a user should always inspect the unit before using it. The specification should state that when a value is needed expressed in a specific unit, toQuanity must be used.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
In https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/FHIRPath/#math-1

In the introductory session at the end of the first sentance add:

> The addition/subtraction of calendar quantities to Date/Time values is documented in the Date/Time Arithmetic section (with link).

 

The N1 specification documents that:

> The unit of the result will be the most granular unit of either input.

We do not propose to change this functionality, however we will add a note to describe how this could be implemented.

> Note: The most granular unit SHALL be determined by comparing the conversion factor of the unit to the UCUM base unit (https://ucum.org/ucum#section-Base-Units), and selecting the largest factor. This approach can also be used to make a determination for more complex units such as velocities. If a specific unit is desired in the result, use [toQuantity(unit)](#fn-toquantity) to specify the unit. e.g. `(1 'cm' + 20 'mm').toQuantity('m')`

item.title: [FHIR-47301] Add a healthcare service element.

item.summary: Add a healthcare service element.

status.description: There is agreement that a change of some sort needs to be made in response to the requested change

comments.comment:
Patient Care FHIR Conference Call on 2/26/2026

Ming shared - From a directory perspective, focus is on non-patient-specific, such as a "diabetic CareTeam" where ACOs think of CareTeam more broadly than hospitals do. 

Russ/Emma confirmed that FHIR CareTeam resource should encompass both types of CareTeams.

Stephen shared Definitional, Proposed and Actual Care Team - Use Case and Scenarios - Learning Health Systems - Confluence

CareTeam.participant.member and CareTeam.participant.onBehalfOf supports reference to HealthcareService

Ming said that the directory was based on FHIR R4 and didn't know HealthcareService had already been added to member/onBehalfOf in R6.

There is also 

CareTeam.category - with example binding - to specify the type of the care team

CareTeam.reason - also exists (CodeableConcept)

Stephen - CareTeam can be virtual - so location isn't specific to the CareTeam.  The location can be referenced via HealthcareService.location

Ming agrees that no changes to the resource are needed, except adding

  1. an example with no patient
  2. a paragraph describing how CareTeam is used when there is no patient

  • 10/4/2025 - Zulip private chat

The focus of this request is to enable people to find a care team that provides specific services and locations, rather than a patient-centric care team. Is the ask to add location at CareTeam or CareTeam.participant level?

Regarding 80/20 rule, do we have any implementers that have a location at the Care Team level?

item.title: [FHIR-55973] (Dis)allow picking the same item multiple times
item.description:
If a question has repeats=true, an answer list, and child items, one can conceive of cases where you would want to pick the same item more than once-- though one could also design the form differently to avoid those cases.  This is about clarifying whether it is the intent that UIs support the ability to pick the same item more than once in the list.  If we do support that, then the next question is whether there are cases where the user should not be able to pick the same item more than once, and whether there should be some setting to control that.

For an example questionnaire and discussion, see this thread:   https://chat.fhir.org/#narrow/channel/179255-questionnaire/topic/selecting.20the.20same.20answer.20more.20than.20once
item.summary: (Dis)allow picking the same item multiple times

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
1. Will add an invariant to QuestionnaireResponse.item that says:

answer.value.isDistinct()

"The same answer cannot be specified more than once for the same question".

 

2. In Questionnaire, will update repeats comment from "If a question is marked as repeats=true, then multiple answers can be provided" to "If a question is marked as repeats=true, then multiple distinct answers can be provided"

and definition from "may include multiple answers" to "may include multiple distinct answers"

3. In the Questionnaire.repeats comments, will move "When repeats=true for a group, it'll be represented with multiple items with the same linkId in the QuestionnaireResponse. For a question, it'll be represented by a single item with that linkId with multiple answers." to be the first paragraph of the comment.

item.title: [FHIR-50763] Use of word record confusing - recommend clarity
item.description:
Change "Once an encrypted Personal Health Record is generated, that has been cryptographically signed, patients, clinicians, and others may have an interest in correcting, amending, or annotating the record, without breaking the cryptographic signature. The SPHR standard offers the Annotation functionality for such cases."  to "Once an encrypted .phr or .sphr file is generated that has been cryptographically signed, patients, clinicians, and others may have an interest in correcting, amending, or annotating the contents, without breaking the cryptographic signature. The PHR FHIR Implementation Guide provides annotation functionality for such cases."

Rationale- this section appears to use Personal Health Record to represent the FHIR based file formatted as .phr or .sphr.  However, Personal Health Record is a term used to represent systems for patients to manage their health information.  Need to be very specific to avoid this ambiguity - otherwise it is confusing.  And it is unclear what SPHR standard is referring to.

 

Also this section refers a lot to IPS with no explanation - this is the first time IPS is mentioned often - it is unclear what the relationship between IPS and the PHR FHIR IG format is.  Need to explain that.   Find it quite confusing.  And also find it hard to determine how annotation is supported in the .phr or .sphr format.  Seems to be showing how it already works with IPS or how to extend IPS but that assumes IPS is being used.  Please remove or make specific to .phr or .sphr.  Would be hard to determine how to implement.
item.summary: Use of word record confusing - recommend clarity

item.title: [FHIR-50735] Reference PHR functional model profile in definition of PHR
item.description:
Recommend referencing the PHR functional model as follows: "For more details on functionality of a personal health record system, the reader is encouraged to review the HL7 PHR System Functional Model profile."  (and provide link)

 

Rationale: no need to redefine the wheel and potentially provide ambiguity between HL7 sources - referencing the PHR system functional model and the Patient Contributed Data White paper help to provide further definition.
item.summary: Reference PHR functional model profile in definition of PHR

item.title: [FHIR-49244] Make scope of the IG more clear. Guide the reader on how to read the IG.
item.description:
After reading the IG I could understand that the IG strongly contributes to:

  • define the content/file formats (Bundle, *.sphr) that can be used to exchange clinical data for PHRs - page: http://hl7.org/fhir/uv/phr/2025Jan/recordkeeping.html
  • define a set of API endpoints - page http://hl7.org/fhir/uv/phr/2025Jan/api.html
  • provide a suggestion on how to work with longitudinal records (use of  Directed Acylic Graphs) - page: http://hl7.org/fhir/uv/phr/2025Jan/longitudinal.html

It took me a significant amount of time to understand the scope/contribution of the IG and how to read it.

Thus, I think it is important to (in the home page):

  • Make scope of the IG more clear.
  • Guide the reader on how to read the IG.
item.summary: Make scope of the IG more clear. Guide the reader on how to read the IG.
